content.problemStatement: Write a program to check whether the three given numbers as input are **Positive, Negative, or Zero**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers. All integers fit in a signed 32‑bit range.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three lines. For each of the three integers, print "Positive" if it is greater than zero, "Negative" if it is less than zero, or "Zero" if it equals zero. The order of the outputs follows the order of the numbers in the input.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. 20 > 0 → Positive, 0 → Zero, -95 < 0 → Negative.
First test case: 5 → Positive, -3 → Negative, 0 → Zero. Second test case: all three numbers are negative.
Three test cases demonstrating mixed signs and zeros.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times and for each iteration read three integers a, b, c. For each of the three values check its sign: if >0 output "Positive", if <0 output "Negative", otherwise output "Zero". Print each result on its own line, preserving the input order. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each integer is examined once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.
